title: Urgineopsis gracilis
taxonomy: Urgineopsis gracilis is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Urgineopsis is a part of the subtribe Coelogyneinae. Urgineopsis gracilis is a species endemic to Borneo. It is a rare and endangered orchid species due to its limited habitat and overcollection.

intro: Urgineopsis gracilis, also known as Urginea maritima or Drimia maritima, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the family Asparagaceae. The health benefits of Urgineopsis gracilis include its use as a traditional treatment for various ailments, such as rheumatism, gout, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include saponins, particularly the glycosides of steroidal alkaloids, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties. Urgineopsis gracilis is commonly used in herbal preparations, including tinctures and infusions, to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
